intro: Tragacantha jessenii, also known as the Jessen's salsify or Tragacanth, is a type of perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its taproot and yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as digestive issues, wounds, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha jessenii include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Tragacantha jessenii include tragacanth gum, a thickening agent used in food and pharmaceutical products, as well as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams.
